alfalfa.definition: A plant, principally of Medicago sativa, grown as a pasture crop.

anvil.definition: A heavy iron block used in the blacksmithing trade as a surface upon which metal can be struck and shaped.

arhat.definition: One who has attained enlightenment; a Buddhist saint.

attach.definition: To fasten, to join to (literally and figuratively).

attack.definition: An attempt to cause damage, injury to, or death of opponent or enemy.

attar.definition: An essential oil extracted from flowers.

avail.definition: Effect in achieving a goal or aim; purpose, use (now usually in negative constructions).

availing.definition: To turn to the advantage of.

bailiff.definition: (law enforcement) An officer of the court, particularly:

biff.definition: A sudden, sharp blow or punch.

carat.definition: A unit of weight for precious stones and pearls, equivalent to 200 milligrams.

cart.definition: A small, open, wheeled vehicle, drawn or pushed by a person or animal, more often used for transporting goods than passengers.

catarrh.definition: Inflammation of the mucous membranes of the nose and throat.

catch.definition: The act of seizing or capturing.

dart.definition: A pointed missile weapon, intended to be thrown by the hand, for example a short lance or javelin

data.definition: (plural: data) A measurement of something on a scale understood by both the recorder (a person or device) and the reader (another person or device). The scale is arbitrarily defined, such as from 1 to 10 by ones, 1 to 100 by 0.1, or simply true or false, on or off, yes, no, or maybe, etc.

fall.definition: The act of moving to a lower position under the effect of gravity.

filial.definition: Pertaining to or befitting a son or daughter.

fill.definition: To occupy fully, to take up all of.

final.definition: A final examination; a test or examination given at the end of a term or class; the test that concludes a class.

finally.definition: At the end or conclusion; ultimately.

finial.definition: The knot or bunch of foliage, or foliated ornament, that forms the upper extremity of a pinnacle in Gothic architecture.

flab.definition: Soft, loose flesh on a person's body; fat.

flabby.definition: Yielding to the touch, and easily moved or shaken; hanging loose by its own weight; lacking firmness; flaccid.

flail.definition: A tool used for threshing, consisting of a long handle with a shorter stick attached with a short piece of chain, thong or similar material.

flan.definition: Baked tart with sweet or savoury filling in an open-topped pastry case. (Compare quiche.)

flyby.definition: A flight past a celestial object in order to make observations.

gallivant.definition: To roam about for pleasure without any definite plan.

gallivanting.definition: To roam about for pleasure without any definite plan.

giving.definition: (ditransitive) To move, shift, provide something abstract or concrete to someone or something or somewhere.

hardhat.definition: A helmet, usually made from rigid plastic, used on construction sites to protect the head from falling objects.

hardtack.definition: A large, hard biscuit made from unleavened flour and water; formerly used as a long-term staple food aboard ships.

hart.definition: A male deer, especially the male of the red deer after his fifth year.

hatch.definition: A horizontal door in a floor or ceiling.

hatrack.definition: A piece of furniture used to store hats and clothing, consisting of a pole with pegs on a moderately broad base; a hatstand.

hill.definition: An elevated location smaller than a mountain.

hilt.definition: The handle of a sword, consisting of grip, guard, and pommel, designed to facilitate use of the blade and afford protection to the hand.

iffy.definition: Of dubious authenticity, legitimacy or legality.

infill.definition: That which fills in a space, hole or gap.

into.definition: To or towards the inside of.

inviting.definition: To ask for the presence or participation of someone or something.

karat.definition: A unit of fineness or concentration of gold equalling 1/24 part of gold in an alloy.

lava.definition: The molten rock ejected by a volcano from its crater or fissured sides.

laving.definition: To pour or throw out, as water; lade out; bail; bail out.

lilt.definition: Animated, brisk motion; spirited rhythm; sprightliness.

limit.definition: A restriction; a bound beyond which one may not go.

limn.definition: To draw or paint; to delineate.

limo.definition: An automobile body with seats and permanent top like a coupe, and with the top projecting over the driver and a projecting front.

lint.definition: A fine material made by scraping cotton or linen cloth; used for dressing wounds.

lion.definition: A big cat, Panthera leo, native to Africa, India and formerly much of Europe.

living.definition: To be alive; to have life.

loin.definition: The part of the body (of humans and quadrupeds) at each side of the backbone, between the ribs and hips

lotion.definition: A low- to medium-viscosity topical preparation intended for application to unbroken skin.

mill.definition: A grinding apparatus for substances such as grains, seeds, etc.

million.definition: (long and short scales) The cardinal number 1,000,000: 106; a thousand thousand.

millionth.definition: The person or thing in the millionth position.

minim.definition: A half note, drawn as a semibreve with a stem.

mint.definition: A building or institution where money (originally, only coins) is produced under government licence.

monolith.definition: A large, single block of stone which is a natural feature; or a block of stone or other similar material used in architecture and sculpture, especially one carved into a monument in ancient times.

motion.definition: A state of progression from one place to another.

naval.definition: Of or relating to a navy.

navigating.definition: To plan, control and record the position and course of a vehicle, ship, aircraft, etc., on a journey; to follow a planned course.

ninth.definition: The person or thing in the ninth position.

notion.definition: Mental apprehension of whatever may be known, thought, or imagined; idea, concept.

olio.definition: A rich, thick, Spanish stew consisting of meat and vegetables.

onion.definition: A monocotyledonous plant (Allium cepa), allied to garlic, used as vegetable and spice.

tack.definition: A small nail with a flat head.

tart.definition: Sharp to the taste; acid; sour.

tartar.definition: A red compound deposited during wine making; mostly potassium hydrogen tartrate - a source of cream of tartar.

that.definition: Something being indicated that is there; one of those.

thatch.definition: Straw, rushes, or similar, used for making or covering the roofs of buildings, or of stacks of hay or grain.

thin.definition: A loss or tearing of paper from the back of a stamp, although not sufficient to create a complete hole.

till.definition: Until; to, up to; as late as (a given time).

titivating.definition: To make small improvements or alterations to (one's appearance etc.); to add some finishing touches to.

toil.definition: Labour, work, especially of a grueling nature.

tomtit.definition: Petroica macrocephala, the miromiro or New Zealand tit, a bird of the Petroicidae (Australasian robin) family.

track.definition: A mark left by something that has passed along.

vagal.definition: Of or relating to the vagus nerve.

vagina.definition: The passage leading from the opening of the vulva to the cervix of the uterus for copulation and childbirth in female mammals.

vaginal.definition: Of, relating to, or affecting the vagina.

vain.definition: Overly proud of oneself, especially concerning appearance; having a high opinion of one's own accomplishments with slight reason.

valiant.definition: A person who acts with valor, showing hero-like characteristics in the midst of danger.

vanilla.definition: Any tropical, climbing orchid of the genus Vanilla (especially Vanilla planifolia), bearing podlike fruit yielding an extract used in flavoring food or in perfumes.

vanillin.definition: A chemical compound, 4-hydroxy-3-methoxybenzaldehyde, that is the primary constituent of vanilla.

vial.definition: A glass vessel or bottle, especially a small tube-shaped bottle used to store medicine, perfume or other chemical.

vigil.definition: An instance of keeping awake during normal sleeping hours, especially to keep watch or pray.

vigilant.definition: Watchful, especially for danger or disorder; alert; wary

villa.definition: (plural "villas") A house, often larger and more expensive than average, in the countryside or on the coast, often used as a retreat.

vital.definition: Relating to, or characteristic of life.

vitiating.definition: To spoil, make faulty; to reduce the value, quality, or effectiveness of something
